name: when-developing-ml-models-use-ml-expert
description: Specialized ML model development, training, and deployment workflow
capabilities: Automated 5-phase ML pipeline from data to deployment
Supports DNN, CNN, RNN, and Transformer architectures
Exports models in Keras, TensorFlow, and TFLite formats
Generates evaluation reports and training visualizations
author: DNYoussef
securitySummary: This skill is a legitimate machine learning development workflow containing standard ML patterns. The 75 static findings are FALSE POSITIVEs: hash identifiers misclassified as C2 keywords, markdown code fences misclassified as Ruby backtick execution, and hash strings misclassified as weak cryptography. All detected operations (file I/O, model serialization, distributed training) are standard ML engineering practices with no malicious intent.
valueStatement: Developing machine learning models requires expertise across data preparation, architecture selection, training, and deployment. This skill automates the entire ML pipeline with best practices built-in, delivering production-ready models in under 90 minutes.
userTitle: Build production-ready ML models with automated workflows
limitations: Input data must be in CSV format
Training time varies based on dataset size and complexity

useCases.title: Rapid Model Prototyping
useCases.description: Quickly train and validate multiple model architectures to find the best performer.

useCases.title: Production Model Deployment
useCases.description: Package trained models with preprocessing pipelines and inference scripts.

useCases.title: Distributed Training
useCases.description: Train large models across multiple nodes with data parallelism.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Classification
promptTemplates.scenario: Train neural network on tabular data
promptTemplates.prompt: Train a deep neural network on 'customer_data.csv' with features age, income, and purchase_history. Target column is 'will_purchase'.

promptTemplates.title: Image Classification
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a CNN model with data augmentation to classify product images into 10 categories from 'product_images.csv'.

promptTemplates.title: Time Series Forecasting
promptTemplates.prompt: Build an LSTM model using 'sales_data.csv' with daily sales figures to forecast next 30 days.

promptTemplates.title: Transfer Learning
promptTemplates.prompt: Fine-tune a pre-trained ResNet model on 'medical_images.csv' for disease classification with frozen early layers.

bestPractices: Always validate data quality before training - clean data produces better models
Monitor training curves for overfitting and adjust dropout or regularization
Test the deployment package locally before pushing to production
antiPatterns: Don't skip data exploration - understanding your data prevents model failures
Avoid training for too many epochs without early stopping to prevent overfitting
Never deploy models without testing on held-out validation data first

faq.question: Which ML frameworks are compatible?
faq.answer: The skill supports both TensorFlow and PyTorch. It automatically detects your environment and uses the appropriate framework.

faq.question: What is the maximum dataset size?
faq.answer: Limited by system memory. For datasets over 1GB, use distributed training with Flow-Nexus to split processing across multiple nodes.

faq.question: Can I integrate with existing MLOps pipelines?
faq.answer: Yes, outputs standard formats (.h5, SavedModel, TFLite) that integrate with MLflow, Kubeflow, and other MLOps platforms.

faq.question: Is my data safe during processing?
faq.answer: All processing happens locally. No data is sent to external services unless you explicitly enable distributed training.

faq.question: What if training fails or performs poorly?
faq.answer: The skill includes automatic retry logic and hyperparameter adjustment. Check the evaluation report for improvement recommendations.

faq.question: How does this compare to AutoML platforms?
faq.answer: Unlike black-box AutoML, this skill provides full transparency with editable code, detailed metrics, and complete control over training.
